text.title: Migration safety: CONCURRENTLY, TIMESTAMPTZ, up/down discipline
text.summary: Migration authoring rules that prevent table locks, timezone bugs, and data loss on production databases.

rule.statement: Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY outside a transaction block for all index creation on tables with existing data. Standard CREATE INDEX takes an exclusive lock that blocks reads and writes for the duration.
rule.rationale: On a table with millions of rows, CREATE INDEX can hold an exclusive lock for minutes, causing a complete outage. CONCURRENTLY builds the index without blocking, at the cost of a longer build time.

antipattern.description: BEGIN; CREATE INDEX idx_... ON large_table(...); COMMIT;
antipattern.reason: CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction. Using it in a BEGIN/COMMIT block silently falls back to the locking version.

rule.statement: Always use TIMESTAMPTZ (TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) for all timestamp columns. Never use TIMESTAMP (without time zone) — it stores values without timezone awareness and causes cross-region data corruption.
rule.rationale: TIMESTAMP stores local time; TIMESTAMPTZ converts to UTC on store and back to session timezone on read. A server that changes timezone (DST, migration) silently mis-interprets TIMESTAMP values.

section.title: Up/down discipline

rule.statement: Every migration must have both an upgrade and a downgrade path. The downgrade must be tested on a clean copy of the database before merging. A no-op downgrade is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a tested downgrade path, a failed deployment cannot be rolled back. CI must apply the migration, then apply the downgrade, and confirm the schema matches the pre-migration state.

section.reference: Alembic migration template with upgrade and downgrade.

rule.statement: ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN NOT NULL DEFAULT ... rewrites the entire table on PostgreSQL versions older than 11 and requires an exclusive lock. On PG 11+ with a non-volatile default it is instant. Confirm the PG version before using this form in production.
rule.rationale: A table rewrite on a 100GB table can take hours and block all access. On older PG, the pattern is: ADD COLUMN nullable → backfill with UPDATE → ADD NOT NULL constraint (deferred).
